refined_fr.refined_fr: Enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the system's capability to measure/report throughput metrics, and (2) the achievement of a customer-defined target. The latter is a success criterion dependent on operational execution, not a system requirement. The refined FR isolates the system's functional obligation—providing visibility into throughput—which is solution-neutral and testable independent of whether a specific productivity target is met. The target itself belongs in acceptance criteria or KPI definitions, not in the FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that the NGHE solution captures and reports material throughput (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations. Verify data accuracy against independent measurement. Confirm reporting is accessible to Site Manager and relevant stakeholders in real time or near-real time.
sub_frs: Acquire material throughput data (volume or mass per time interval) during equipment operation.
Calculate and aggregate throughput metrics from acquired data.
Report throughput metrics to authorized stakeholders in human-readable format.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous measurement of material mass or volume per unit time, aggregate over evaluation period, and compute average throughput against target baseline.

DP2: Measure cumulative material quantity (mass or volume) at discrete time intervals, calculate differential quantities, derive instantaneous rates, and compare period-average against productivity_target.

DP3: Instrument material stream with real-time state sensors (position, velocity, density proxies), infer flow rate through kinematic or mass-balance models, log aggregated throughput, and validate against acceptance criteria.

DP4: Establish reference material batches of known quantity, measure time-to-completion for each batch, compute batch throughput, aggregate across evaluation period, and compare to productivity_target.

DP5: Deploy dual independent measurement channels (e.g., mass-based and volume-based) with cross-validation logic; report throughput only when both channels agree within tolerance; flag discrepancies for investigation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Measure cumulative material quantity (mass or volume) at discrete time intervals, calculate differential quantities, derive instantaneous rates, and compare period-average against productivity_target.

potential_sources: Interval sampling frequency must be chosen to balance temporal resolution against measurement noise and computational overhead—risk of aliasing if intervals too coarse, or noise amplification if too fine.

Differential calculation (Δquantity / Δtime) introduces sensitivity to measurement precision at interval boundaries; cumulative error can propagate if intervals are not synchronized with operational state changes.
Acceptance criterion (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target) couples the measurement DP to an external baseline; if baseline is not independently validated, reported compliance may be spurious.
DP2 assumes material flow is quasi-continuous; if flow is highly transient or bursty, discrete sampling may miss peak rates or underestimate variability.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 is selected because it decouples the measurement mechanism (cumulative quantity acquisition) from the rate derivation logic (differential calculation), allowing independent evolution of sensor technology and post-processing algorithms. It avoids the complexity of real-time flow sensors (DP1, DP3) while remaining robust to sensor drift via cumulative measurement. DP4 (batch-based) is simpler but less responsive to operational variability. DP5 (dual-channel) adds redundancy but increases cost and complexity without addressing the core measurement challenge. DP1 requires continuous flow sensors, which may not be available or cost-effective for all material types.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Material quantity can be measured (or inferred) at discrete intervals with acceptable precision. Risk: If measurement uncertainty is large relative to Δquantity, derived rates will be unreliable. Mitigation: Specify minimum interval duration and measurement accuracy requirements.

Assumption: Evaluation period is long enough to average out transient fluctuations and operational noise. Risk: Short evaluation windows may not capture representative steady-state throughput. Mitigation: Define minimum evaluation period (e.g., ≥ 8 operational hours or ≥ 100 batches).

Assumption: productivity_target is independently validated and reflects realistic operational conditions. Risk: If target is set without baseline data, acceptance criteria may be unachievable or trivial. Mitigation: Establish productivity_target via pilot runs or historical data before formal evaluation.

Assumption: Material flow is not significantly affected by environmental factors (temperature, humidity, density variation) that would invalidate mass-to-volume conversions. Risk: If material properties vary, reported throughput may be biased. Mitigation: Specify measurement units (mass or volume) and environmental conditions for evaluation.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan permits installation of measurement instrumentation without disrupting operations or creating hazards. Risk: If measurement points are inaccessible or unsafe, DP2 cannot be implemented. Mitigation: Conduct site survey and safety review before design finalization.

Assumption: Discrete sampling intervals are synchronized with operational state (e.g., material feed start/stop, equipment mode changes). Risk: If sampling is asynchronous, transient effects may be missed or double-counted. Mitigation: Define sampling trigger logic (time-based, event-based, or hybrid).
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly specify: (1) measurement units (mass, volume, or both); (2) required temporal resolution (e.g., instantaneous rate vs. period-average); (3) acceptable measurement uncertainty (e.g., ±5% of reported throughput); (4) evaluation period duration and operational conditions (e.g., steady-state, mixed-mode, transient); (5) definition of productivity_target (baseline, specification, or derived from pilot data). Additionally, decompose the FR into two sub-FRs: FR2a (Acquire cumulative material quantity) and FR2b (Compute and report throughput metrics), to enable independent DP selection and IM assignment for measurement hardware vs. data processing logic.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized productivity monitoring and rate-of-work analytics engine that aggregates material quantity measurements, computes differential rates, and enforces productivity targets across all operational modes.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives discrete material quantity samples (mass or volume) at regular intervals from Vehicle sensors and operational subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outputs productivity performance feedback and target-compliance status to guide task scheduling and operational adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Triggers alerts if productivity falls below threshold, signaling potential operational anomalies or safety-relevant performance degradation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE Solution is the logical home for system-level analytics, rate computation, and target comparison. Measurement aggregation, time-series analysis, and threshold enforcement are core to a centralized monitoring engine. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion and alert/tasking outputs cross boundaries.

Poor fit. Vehicle is a physical platform; placing analytics logic here couples computation tightly to hardware and creates maintenance burden. Rate derivation and target comparison are system-level concerns, not vehicle-specific. High diffusion: logic would need to be replicated or exposed across multiple vehicle instances.

Weak fit. Teleoperation handles remote command and control; productivity analytics are orthogonal to operator interface concerns. Placing DP here conflates control logic with monitoring logic. High diffusion: analytics would leak into operator UI and command processing, violating separation of concerns.

Moderate fit. Safety Services could consume productivity alerts (SafetyAlert output), but should not own the measurement, rate calculation, or target comparison logic. Productivity monitoring is a distinct concern from safety enforcement. Moderate diffusion: Safety Services would depend on external rate data, creating coupling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP (cumulative measurement, rate derivation, and target comparison) is a system-level analytics function that belongs in a centralized, domain-agnostic monitoring engine. NGHE Solution is the appropriate boundary because it is positioned to ingest telemetry from all sources, perform time-series analysis, and emit actionable feedback (tasking and safety alerts) without being constrained by vehicle hardware, operator interface, or safety-specific logic.
containment_risks: If telemetry ingestion latency is high or variable, rate calculations may become inaccurate; mitigation: define strict telemetry sampling contract and buffer strategy.

If multiple Vehicle instances operate concurrently, NGHE Solution must aggregate and disaggregate per-vehicle productivity; mitigation: design DP to support multi-instance tracking with clear vehicle identity tagging.
If productivity_target is dynamic or context-dependent, DP must support target updates; mitigation: expose target as a configurable parameter, not hardcoded.

If Safety Services and Tasking subsystems have conflicting interpretations of productivity alerts, coordination overhead increases; mitigation: define clear semantics for SafetyAlert and Tasking outputs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criterion 'reported productivity ≥ productivity_target over evaluation period' is directly measurable from NGHE Solution outputs. Telemetry ingestion from Vehicle sensors provides ground truth for cumulative material quantity; differential rate calculation and target comparison are deterministic and auditable. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of telemetry sampling contract, (2) logging of rate derivations and threshold comparisons, (3) post-evaluation reconciliation against worksite records.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR is allocated to NGHE Solution as the single authoritative owner of measurement aggregation, rate derivation, and target enforcement. Vehicle provides raw telemetry input; Tasking and SafetyAlert consume outputs. No ambiguity in responsibility boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP2 is functionally complete and directly addresses FR. It specifies the complete measurement-to-reporting pipeline: (1) cumulative quantity sampling at discrete intervals, (2) differential rate calculation, (3) period-average derivation, (4) target comparison. The DP is expressed in domain-agnostic terms (mass/volume, time intervals, instantaneous rates) and does not prematurely constrain implementation technology. Traceability is explicit: FR→DP mapping is one-to-one with no functional gaps.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry sampling contract risk: If Vehicle sensors emit samples at variable intervals or with latency jitter, rate calculations become sensitive to timing assumptions. Mitigation: Define strict telemetry schema (timestamp precision, sampling frequency, data freshness SLA) and enforce in NGHE Solution ingestion layer. Acceptance criterion depends on consistent sampling; document sampling assumptions in verification plan.

Multi-vehicle aggregation risk: If NGHE Solution must track productivity per-vehicle while operating a fleet, identity tagging and per-vehicle state management must be explicit in DP realization. Risk of cross-vehicle data leakage or aggregation errors. Mitigation: Design DP with vehicle_id as a first-class parameter; validate isolation in unit and integration tests.

Dynamic target risk: If productivity_target is context-dependent (e.g., varies by terrain, material type, operational mode), hardcoding target in NGHE Solution creates maintenance burden and violates configurability. Mitigation: Expose productivity_target as a runtime parameter with versioning; document target update protocol and impact on ongoing rate comparisons.

Alert semantics ambiguity: SafetyAlert and Tasking outputs both consume productivity feedback but may interpret threshold breach differently (e.g., SafetyAlert triggers on single breach; Tasking triggers on trend). Risk of conflicting corrective actions. Mitigation: Define explicit alert semantics (threshold definition, hysteresis, debounce logic) in DP specification; validate coordination with Safety Services and Tasking owners.

Measurement accuracy dependency: DP assumes telemetry provides accurate cumulative quantity. If Vehicle sensors have systematic bias or drift, reported productivity will be systematically incorrect. Mitigation: Establish sensor calibration and validation protocol; document measurement uncertainty bounds in acceptance criteria.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score justified). NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because: (1) it is domain-agnostic and can ingest telemetry from any Vehicle configuration, (2) rate derivation and target comparison are system-level analytics, not vehicle-specific logic, (3) centralization avoids duplication and ensures consistent productivity semantics across all operational modes, (4) external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking/SafetyAlert out) are clean and minimal. No evidence of DP diffusion into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Ownership is unambiguous. Minimal diffusion score (0.08) reflects only necessary boundary crossings (telemetry ingestion and alert emission), which are architectural by design, not logic leakage.

recommended_revisions: Formalize telemetry sampling contract: Define schema for material quantity samples (units, precision, timestamp format, sampling frequency, maximum latency). Add to DP specification as a precondition for rate calculation accuracy.

Extend DP to specify multi-vehicle tracking: Add vehicle_id parameter to rate calculation and target comparison logic. Document per-vehicle state isolation and aggregation rules if fleet-level reporting is required.

Parameterize productivity_target: Revise DP to treat productivity_target as a configurable input (not hardcoded). Specify target update protocol and impact on ongoing rate comparisons (e.g., retroactive vs. prospective application).

Define alert semantics explicitly: Clarify SafetyAlert and Tasking output conditions (threshold definition, hysteresis, debounce, trend detection). Document coordination protocol with Safety Services and Tasking subsystems to prevent conflicting corrective actions.

Document measurement uncertainty: Add acceptance criterion clause specifying acceptable sensor accuracy and calibration requirements. Define how measurement bias or drift affects productivity reporting and target compliance verification.

Add buffer/queue strategy to DP: Specify how NGHE Solution handles telemetry ingestion bursts, out-of-order samples, or temporary sensor unavailability. Define impact on rate calculation continuity and alert latency.

Specify rate calculation window: Clarify whether 'instantaneous rates' are computed as rolling averages, exponential smoothing, or raw differentials. Define window size and update frequency. This affects sensitivity to short-term fluctuations vs. trend detection.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence, but the recommended_revisions and diffusion_risks expose critical design dependencies that must be decomposed into explicit sub-FRs before implementation. The DP is functionally complete but operationally underspecified: telemetry sampling contract, multi-vehicle state isolation, dynamic target parameterization, alert semantics coordination, and measurement uncertainty handling are all architectural concerns that warrant dedicated functional requirements. Safety criticality (high) elevates the importance of explicit decomposition rather than implicit assumptions in implementation. Proceeding to sub-level decomposition ensures these concerns are elevated to first-class requirements with clear ownership and verification criteria, rather than buried in implementation details or mitigation notes.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2.1: Ingest and validate telemetry samples from Vehicle with guaranteed schema compliance and timing precision (timestamp, material quantity, sampling frequency, freshness SLA).
FR2.2: Isolate and track productivity state per vehicle_id to prevent cross-vehicle data leakage and enable fleet-level aggregation without identity confusion.

FR2.3: Accept productivity_target as a runtime-configurable parameter with versioning and document the protocol for target updates and their retroactive/prospective application to ongoing rate comparisons.

FR2.4: Define and enforce alert output semantics (threshold breach conditions, hysteresis, debounce logic, trend detection rules) for SafetyAlert and Tasking consumers to prevent conflicting corrective actions.
FR2.5: Establish measurement accuracy requirements and sensor calibration protocol, including acceptable bias/drift bounds and impact on productivity reporting validity.
FR2.6: Implement telemetry buffer/queue strategy to handle ingestion bursts, out-of-order samples, and temporary sensor unavailability without disrupting rate calculation continuity or alert latency.

FR2.7: Specify instantaneous rate calculation method (rolling average window size, exponential smoothing parameters, or raw differential) and update frequency to balance sensitivity to short-term fluctuations vs. trend detection.

revise.why: DP2 is logically sound but operationally underspecified. Before decomposing sub-FRs, revise DP2 to explicitly incorporate the recommended_revisions as preconditions and design constraints: (1) formalize telemetry sampling contract as a DP input specification, (2) add vehicle_id as a first-class parameter in rate calculation and state management, (3) treat productivity_target as a configurable input with versioning semantics, (4) specify alert output conditions (threshold, hysteresis, debounce) as DP outputs, (5) document measurement uncertainty bounds as acceptance criteria, (6) add buffer/queue strategy for telemetry ingestion, (7) specify rate calculation window and update frequency. This revision elevates DP2 from a logical sketch to an operationally complete design that can be decomposed into sub-DPs with clear interfaces and dependencies.

assumptions: Vehicle telemetry is emitted at consistent intervals with negligible latency jitter; if not, rate calculations will be biased.
Productivity_target is static during evaluation period; if dynamic (context-dependent), DP must parameterize target as runtime input.
SafetyAlert and Tasking subsystems have compatible interpretations of productivity threshold breach; if not, alert semantics must be explicitly coordinated.
Vehicle sensors provide accurate cumulative material quantity with acceptable calibration; if systematic bias exists, reported productivity will be systematically incorrect.
NGHE Solution is the sole authoritative owner of productivity measurement and reporting; no other subsystem duplicates or overrides this logic.
Multi-vehicle fleet operation is in scope; if single-vehicle operation only, per-vehicle state isolation is not required.
open_risks: Telemetry sampling contract risk (HIGH): Variable sampling intervals or latency jitter will introduce bias into rate calculations. Mitigation: Define strict telemetry schema and enforce in NGHE Solution ingestion layer. Verification: Instrument sampling contract compliance and log timing deviations.

Multi-vehicle aggregation risk (MEDIUM): Cross-vehicle data leakage or identity confusion if vehicle_id is not a first-class parameter. Mitigation: Design DP with explicit per-vehicle state isolation. Verification: Unit and integration tests for vehicle_id isolation.

Dynamic target risk (MEDIUM): Hardcoded productivity_target creates maintenance burden and violates configurability. Mitigation: Expose target as runtime parameter with versioning. Verification: Document target update protocol and impact on ongoing comparisons.

Alert semantics ambiguity (HIGH, safety-critical): SafetyAlert and Tasking may interpret threshold breach differently, leading to conflicting corrective actions. Mitigation: Define explicit alert semantics (threshold, hysteresis, debounce, trend detection). Verification: Coordinate with Safety Services and Tasking owners; validate alert output conditions in integration tests.

Measurement accuracy dependency (HIGH, safety-critical): Sensor bias or drift will systematically corrupt productivity reporting. Mitigation: Establish sensor calibration and validation protocol. Verification: Document measurement uncertainty bounds and acceptance criteria.

Telemetry buffer/queue strategy (MEDIUM): Ingestion bursts, out-of-order samples, or temporary sensor unavailability may disrupt rate calculation continuity. Mitigation: Design buffer/queue strategy with clear overflow and recovery semantics. Verification: Stress test with simulated telemetry anomalies.

Rate calculation window ambiguity (MEDIUM): Unclear whether instantaneous rates are rolling averages, exponential smoothing, or raw differentials. Affects sensitivity to short-term fluctuations. Mitigation: Specify window size and update frequency in DP. Verification: Validate rate calculation against reference data.
evidence_needed: Telemetry schema specification (timestamp precision, sampling frequency, data freshness SLA, units, precision for material quantity).
Vehicle sensor calibration and validation protocol (acceptable bias/drift bounds, measurement uncertainty quantification).
Productivity_target versioning and update protocol (retroactive vs. prospective application, impact on ongoing rate comparisons).
Alert semantics specification (threshold definition, hysteresis, debounce logic, trend detection rules for SafetyAlert and Tasking).
Coordination protocol with Safety Services and Tasking subsystems (conflict resolution, alert precedence, corrective action sequencing).
Telemetry buffer/queue strategy (overflow handling, out-of-order sample recovery, temporary unavailability resilience).
Rate calculation method specification (rolling average window size, exponential smoothing parameters, or raw differential; update frequency).
Per-vehicle state isolation design (vehicle_id parameter, state management, aggregation rules for fleet-level reporting).
Integration test plan for multi-vehicle scenarios, telemetry anomalies, and alert coordination.
Measurement accuracy acceptance criteria and sensor validation test results.
